pi_items.source: task.json metadata; confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh
task.json metadata; confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh

Task description states 'Minimum Hounsfield Unit (HU) threshold of at least 1500 HU'; medical literature confirms enamel threshold is >1500 HU. Metadata value of 1400 contradicts the stated requirement.
Anatomical reference: complete human dentition (32 teeth) = 5,000-8,000 mm³. Metadata value aligns with typical tooth set volume.
Anatomical reference: petrous bone + jaw structures + teeth can total 100,000-150,000 mm³; full skull is >350,000 mm³. Metadata value is reasonable for teeth + dense bone without full skull.

Downloaded from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip. Verified as real multi-file DICOM series (108 CT slices) with consistent Study/Series UIDs. Patient: 'CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample', Study Date: 2002-06-04. Hounsfield Unit range: -1024 to 3033 HU (matches task description 'range e.g., 1500 to 3071 HU').

privileged_info_summary: The CT Cranium dataset (0051) is real public medical imaging data from the official InVesalius 3 release repository, consisting of 108 CT slices of a human cranium with Hounsfield Unit range -1024 to 3033. The task requires segmentation of teeth enamel and dense petrous bone using a Hounsfield Unit threshold. The task description specifies 'at least 1500 HU' as the threshold, which aligns with medical literature (enamel >1500 HU). However, the metadata specifies min_hu_threshold of 1400, which contradicts the stated requirement and makes the task easier than described. Volume constraints of 5,000-250,000 mm³ are anatomically reasonable for isolating dentition and dense bone while excluding the full skull (>350,000 mm³).
